title: Languas microlophon
taxonomy: Languas microlophon is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae, commonly known as the ginger family. It is a member of the genus Languas, which is native to Southeast Asia. The Zingiberaceae family comprises over 1,600 species of plants, including various types of ginger, cardamom, and galangal. These plants are characterized by their distinctive inflorescence and aromatic properties. The classification of Languas microlophon is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.
